description: A differential interface fails emissions or link tests because intra-pair length mismatch converts signal into common-mode noise.
body: Failure mode: the two traces of a pair differ in length so their signals arrive skewed, producing common-mode current that radiates and degrades the link. Detection signals: common-mode emissions on the pair, link errors at higher rates, and a measured intra-pair skew above the budget. Do not add a common-mode choke as the only remedy while the skew is uncorrected. Safer fallback: tune the shorter trace at the point of mismatch to match lengths within the budget and re-verify skew and emissions.
